review_title: Great Learning Tool
description: Full LMS training of over 1000 employees across the continental United States allows me to accurately track who has and who has not taken training. And the ability to create training when needed or modify existing training on the fly is a huge plus.
author: Peter S.
pros: It is very easy to create training from scratch using the embedded features in Bridge or import the training materials from other authoring software. The tracking capabilities to know down to the IP address of the machine (type of) that accessed the training and when that took place is a great benefit as well.
cons: I cannot think of anything at this time.

review_title: Easy to Use as Admin; Great Support from Bridge Staff
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - The Bridge staff ROCKS! Everyone we worked with during implementation was super helpful and went out of their way to help us whenever possible. They even gave us a tour of their office and took us out for drinks when we were in Salt Lake City. - The tools are EXTREMELY easy to use. No big learning curve at all for user management, course creation, and other necessary tasks - They have an Ideas Page where anyone can log ideas on how to improve Bridge and it seems as if many of the ideas have been incorporated into the roadmap
cons: - The course authorship can be lacking in functionality at times. Any kind of robust font, table creation, etc cannot currently be done within the tool - There is a lack of customizability in certain places

review_title: Not a pretty interface, not a lot of options or usefulness
description: Sometimes I get assigned a course by accident and can't get rid of the notifications that I'm "late" on completing something I shouldn't have to take in the first place! I don't like that the administrator has to control everything within this software to get anything real done.
author: Brittany U.
pros: I like that you are able to use this platform to distribute learning tools to large groups of people and that they are all able to complete the training on their own time and on their own devices.
cons: I don't like the user interface, it seems almost too simple (very bare). Also, it's hard to assign and un-assign courses to individuals within the organization.

review_title: Bridge Sets You Up For Success
description: Bridge has helped our organization improve communication regarding required trainings and timelines for completion. We were using excel sheets for tracking that took exorbitant amounts of time. With Bridge, all that tracking is now automated and we have more time to focus on course development and employee engagement.
author: Cassie D.
pros: The software is very user friendly and easy to navigate. The course builder is simple and clean. The automatic reminder emails are very helpful for employees and managers.
cons: There are a few things that I would tweak if given the opportunity: display the steps in a program when you open up the program on the author page - currently you must edit the program to see the different steps. It would also be really helpful if there was an option to take attendance for each day of a multi-day live training.
author_profession: Associate Director of Learning & Employee Development
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great for professional development
description: My department in particular wants to deploy instruction throughout the year, and make it high-quality, and that's exactly what we've been able to do.
author: Michael A.
pros: A lot of software for online learning is geared toward students, which makes it difficult to do for a company for professional development. Bridge specifically is built for professional development, which is made it easier for us to implement (instead of shoehorning a student-focused piece of software). They are also pretty responsive to community requests for new features.
cons: Classes that aren't assigned to a particular learner (i.e. optional ones) go in the Learning Library, but searching is difficult unless you know the class title or the class creator has put some good tags to make it more searchable.

review_title: Great Company-Wide Tool
author: Andy M.
pros: I love that you can house it all here. Our organization utilizes Bridge for our company-wide compliance trainings and any other all-company types of training/learning necessary for annual completion (i.e. Employee handbook review and signature).
cons: We do not experience regular negatives with this particular software.

review_title: Bridge
description: I really liked it when I had the opportunity to use it. Since my company merged, less focus has been put on the internal knowledge programs. But I believe once the waters settle, we will get back into the internal knowledge programs.
author: Rachel F.
pros: It's easy to use and navigate. I like learning from the cartoons and it simplifies sections of interest.
cons: Sometimes the classes on my bridge account are not offered. This is more of a personal work problem than the software itself because my company hasn't paid for that particular subscription.

review_title: In-house training with Bridge
description: I think Bridge is a great solution for creating and maintaining on in-house/onsite training program.
author: Abe Y.
pros: Bridge gives us the ability to develop an in-house training program which increases our business continuity capability. It also allows us to track training progress in our staff.
cons: Adoption and use by our staff has been slower than hoped for.

review_title: Good course platform for enterprise training
description: It has a simple and clean interface, and it is very customizable. Maybe some navigation could be improved.
author: Danilo N.
pros: It shows your courses in a simple way, it is easy to see what courses you already did, those that are pending, etc. Its left menu bar is very useful too. Besides that, it is very customizable, so you can put custom tools on it, to add your own organization tools/links.
cons: It could have a simpler way to navigate through course modules because it is less intuitive, so you end being on the homepage again, needing to find the course, open the next module, which adds a few boring extra steps.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bridge is great for large teams
description: Learned a lot through the courses I have taken and I love that it is standardized across my org so perfectly.
author: Hayden A.
pros: I love how Bridge will track progress so seamlessly and save where you are at in any given course. The UI is clean and user-friendly so I give it an overall thumbs up.
cons: At times I would be asked to repeat a step in a course that was already passed. This may have been a user error on my part.

review_title: Overall User friendly
description: Overall it was user friendly for me and my colleagues
author: Rhema F.
pros: It was user friendly for the most part. It was helpful to have training at individualized pace.
cons: At times it was a tad bit difficult checking was trainings were pending.

review_title: Bridge LMS combines ease of use with great service to provide an excellent e-learning solution
description: Is Bridge perfect? No. But it provides great value, intuitive use, and a lot of excellent features, all backed by a great support team. And the fact that there is an attitude of continuous improvement with a strong emphasis on community involvement makes it an LMS that will grow based on real needs from users, not perceived needs from a marketing team that is out of touch with its users.
author: Earl P.
pros: Bridge LMS has a very intuitive interface, and provides the ability to tailor user roles to meet virtually any need. Getting up to speed in using it was very easy, and yet I am continually impressed with the "and you can do this, too?" factor, especially when it comes to managing courses and users. In addition, the customer service team at Bridge, combined with an excellent support community, makes it easy to find solutions to challenges - and even provide recommendations for new features and functionality. The ability to author responsive courses within the LMS, or import SCORM, AICC, and Powerpoint files also provides flexibility on getting content to learners. And the overall value is excellent compared to many other LMS options I considered and tested.
cons: Many of the drawbacks I have found are already in the queue for updates and improvements, such as not being able to duplicate whole programs, but instead having to do it on a course-by-course basis. A couple things I have found that I hope make it to future upgrades, however, include: 1) Improved customization of the pages, especially in sub-accounts. There is some limited ability to customize logos and accent colors throughout the interface, but not much. Likewise, layout options in courses authored within Bridge are also somewhat limited, with no ability to wrap text or customize fonts. 2) Meta tags applied to courses on the master account do not carry to sub-accounts. While I understand the reasoning (users in that sub-account may search by completely different parameters), it would be nice to have the option of sharing the meta tags, if desired. 3) Inability to export course content authored in Bridge back out into some sort of usable format, which is a common trait of most LMS authoring tools I have seen.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great LMS
description: We use Bridge to organize both online and instructor-led training. We assign modules using Programs for advancement programs. We use the development area for quick modules or quizzes. Overall, we appreciate the ease of functionality of the program.
author: James B.
pros: Instructure is constantly updating their programs. This adds new functionality on a regular basis and changes things without needing a complete new version to get used to.
cons: There are a couple things that have added unnecessary clicks but still very few overall.

review_title: Great user experience & customer support - minimalist design
description: I have been using Bridge for entrepreneuriship upskilling in a blended mode & find it easy to use as a course author. Learners have minimal learning curve and it is mobile friendly too. The Programs and Live Trainings features allow us to create blended curriculum, which is extremely important for us.
author: Ramya S.
pros: - Ease of use (for a learner) - Great UI and UX - Supports Blended Learning use cases
cons: - Insights & asnalytics are not sufficient & not much raw data is available for customized dashboards

review_title: Bridge to your organisation learning needs
author: Murray H.
pros: Bridge's functionality and its usability for the design of online learning makes the role for administrator easy for RA to deliver: - Compliance training - Training and assessment of Nationally Recognised Training - Social learning Our learners appreciate the interface and simplicity when using the system. Bridge's community is also an asset when seeking assistance to enhance your organisation's knowledge of the system.
cons: The improvement our organisation has requested is the questioning features to assist our learning strategy in delivering content to our staff.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: An LMS created for the end user.
description: They are really great, and have a modern take on learning, career development, and performance tracking. I think at least a demo from them is worth ANY company's time.
author: Andrew Z.
pros: It is simple to use, has beautiful design, and includes functional and groundbreaking features to cover the expanse of employee progression. It can be administrated by a much smaller team, and has the most amazing training tool ever created, called "Practice".
cons: Some of our data integration is a little messy, and required significant work-arounds to develop. Other than that, they are ideal for our business.

review_title: LMS
author: Julye C.
pros: The ease of use for an admin and the learner
cons: Wish there were folders in the author library so it was easier to sort

review_title: Bridge LMS is a winner!
description: When our group decided to produce an education program we had certain criteria which we knew would be a must for the LMS we would ultimately select. There are an overwhelming number of possibilities and pricing is all over the map. We settled on Bridge as it checked all the necessary boxes. Response from the staff at Bridge was quick and every question was answered to our satisfaction. What we didn't realize initially was that support was going to be such an important part of the LMS selection. Bridge support has been with us every step of the way. Our project will be done soon. I would highly recommend the Bridge LMS platform!
author: Larry L.
pros: Bridge is professional and functional and will provide easy updates as needed in the future.
cons: No negative comments regarding Bridge.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: It is a good quality LMS for corporate training.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This LMS is really useful if you are in the corporate learning management business. It categorizes your users using lots of filters. It could be integrated with ERP and CRM infrastructure which is super useful for corporations. But the best feature may be the analytics it could bring. And it is cloud-based!
cons: Its UX is not so great, a bit confusing at some points. Management could be tricky. It only supports English language.

review_title: Using Bridge to Scale Training to Customers
author: Giovanna C.
pros: We love the simplicity of the interface and the ease of use. Bridge is very intuitive.
cons: Our use case of this platform is a little different from the way it was intended. We don't use it for corporate training, but rather to train our customers on the use of our software. Our customers are educators who need clock hours to advance in their career and document their PD. Bridge generates certificates, but they are not customizable. I wish Bridge would automatically generate certificates that that include the number of clock hours and a signature from the trainers.

review_title: Bridge is taking the industry by storm!
description: My favorite part about Bridge is their customer service. Best customer service I have ever received with any software! Online chat support is top notch. They own every problem and solve most in a matter of minutes. Bridge has many great features. Pros- Customer Service is fantastic! It's easy to learn From an Admin perspective, it is simple and fast From the learner perspective, it is easy nice looking It works very well on mobile devices LMS for the future Constantly adding new features Authoring is very basic and easy to learn Cons- Authoring tool is very basic. Most authors use external tools, which is very common. But don't expect the authoring tool to have the same fuctionality as Captivate, Articulate, Lectora, etc... You can upload those courses very easily however.
author: Ben L.

review_title: Bridge by Instructure - Non-profit Sector Review
description: Fantastic from sales pitch, to sandbox, contract process, training, customer support and implementation.
author: Cristian C.
pros: Intuitive UI, simplicity for authoring courses and enrolling users, relevant student reporting, great training and customer support.
cons: CSV based group user management - would prefer to have this built in to the web site itself rather than having to do so via CSV import
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Review of demo
description: At this point I have only demo'd the product, but the Bridge system seems very easy to navigate and work in.
author: Missy J.
pros: Simple and easy to maneuver through. I feel end users would be able to learn the system very easily.
cons: Bridge is new and there is some functionality not there yet. I think this functionality would be developed in the future, I just don't know what their time frames are like.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bridge is more than a Bridge!
author: David P.
pros: The software is easy to use and very intuitive. It bridge's a gap for employees to help them become smarter, better informed and more productive.
cons: They are so much better than just a year ago. new features and updates are being updated all the time. They are headed in the right direction and seem to read our minds on what is needed next.

review_title: A good LMS tool
description: it is a decent lms tool which provides some good features. It has a great and easy to use interface. Very user friendly but it lacks in features department. Need to increase functionality.
author: Karan U.
